basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Taghābun — “Mutual Loss and Gain” — takes its name from the Day of Gathering, called yawm al-taghābun, the day of the great exchange of regrets: each one discovers what he might have gained and what he lost, the saved no less than the damned. The sūra opens with the universal glorification — all that is in the heavens and the earth proclaims His glory — and unfolds the divine knowledge that embraces the hidden and the manifest. Qushayrī meditates on the forming of man (wa-ṣawwarakum fa-aḥsana ṣuwarakum) as the shaping of the outer by power and the inner by nearness; and he makes “no calamity strikes save by God's leave” the key to the guidance of the heart: whoever believes, God guides his heart — in ease through gratitude, in trial through patience. The sūra culminates in the goodly loan made to God (qarḍ ḥasan): the rich give of their wealth, the poor give the stripping-bare of their own will.
themes: the mutual loss and gain of the Gathering
calamity and the guidance of the heart

versets.ar: يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ ۖ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ ۖ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: yusabbiḥu lillāhi mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi lahu l-mulku wa-lahu l-ḥamdu wa-huwa ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: All that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth glorifies God. His is the Kingdom and His is the Praise! He is powerful over all things!
versets.commentaire: All things proclaim His glory (tasbīḥ): the speech of beings is an unbroken praise. Qushayrī notes that this song fills the universe, but the deaf of denial do not hear it — creation praises ceaselessly, and only forgetfulness renders one mute to this concert. His is the Kingdom (mulk) He holds, His the Praise (ḥamd) due to Him: to acknowledge the one is to join the other.

mots.racine: س ب ح
mots.def: sabbaḥa: to proclaim God's transcendence, to declare Him free of all defect.

mots.ar: مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ
mots.tr: mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍ
mots.racine: س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: what is in the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: م ل ك / ح م د
mots.sens: His is the Kingdom and the Praise

mots.racine: ق د ر

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ فَمِنكُمْ كَافِرٌ وَمِنكُم مُّؤْمِنٌ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī khalaqakum fa-minkum kāfirun wa-minkum muʾminun wa-llāhu bimā taʿmalūna baṣīrun
versets.trad: It is He who created you; yet among you is one who denies and one who believes. And God sees perfectly what you do!
versets.commentaire: From a single creation two destinies are born: denial and faith. Qushayrī reads here the mystery of the antecedent allotment (qisma): God creates all men by one hand, but the secret of faith and denial reaches back to a decree prior to the deed. And He remains baṣīr, seeing the depth of acts: the gaze that creates is the gaze that scrutinizes.

mots.racine: خ ل ق
mots.sens: it is He who created you

mots.racine: ك ف ر / أ م ن
mots.sens: among you a denier, a believer
mots.def: kāfir: one who denies; muʾmin: one who believes.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ب ص ر

versets.ar: خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِّ وَصَوَّرَكُمْ فَأَحْسَنَ صُوَرَكُمْ ۖ وَإِلَيْهِ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-ṣawwarakum fa-aḥsana ṣuwarakum wa-ilayhi l-maṣīru
versets.trad: He created the heavens and the earth in Truth. And He shaped you, giving you a harmonious form; and to Him is the return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates the twofold shaping: God shaped (ṣawwara) the outer of man by His power, and fashioned his inner by His nearness. The fair form (aḥsana ṣuwarakum) is not only the beauty of the body: it is the capacity to bear the secret of gnosis. And all is strained toward the return (maṣīr): the form received is but a loan on the way back to the One who gave it.

mots.racine: ح ق ق
ص و ر
ح س ن / ص و ر
mots.def: aḥsana: to make beautiful; ṣuwar: forms.

mots.racine: ص ي ر

versets.ar: يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تُسِرُّونَ وَمَا تُعْلِنُونَ ۚ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ
versets.tr: yaʿlamu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-yaʿlamu mā tusirrūna wa-mā tuʿlinūna wa-llāhu ʿalīmun bi-dhāti ṣ-ṣudūri
versets.trad: He knows what is in the heavens and on the earth, and He knows what you hide and what you reveal. God knows perfectly the contents of hearts.
versets.commentaire: The divine knowledge embraces both worlds and both registers: the secret (sirr) and the open (ʿalāniyya). Qushayrī: before the One who knows the contents of the breasts, hypocrisy has no hiding-place, and the sincere has nothing to fear. To know that God knows the depths of hearts turns outer modesty into inner vigilance (murāqaba).

mots.racine: س ر ر / ع ل ن
mots.def: asarra: to keep secret; aʿlana: to declare openly.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ص د ر
mots.sens: knower of the contents of breasts

versets.ar: أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ نَبَأُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِن قَبْلُ فَذَاقُوا وَبَالَ أَمْرِهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: a-lam yaʾtikum nabaʾu lladhīna kafarū min qablu fa-dhāqū wabāla amrihim wa-lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: Has the tidings not come to you of those who denied long ago and tasted the consequences of their conduct, suffering a painful punishment?
versets.commentaire: The story of past peoples is a mirror held up to the present. Qushayrī: he who sows thorns reaps no roses — they tasted the bitterness (wabāl) of their own conduct. The tidings (nabaʾ) of the perished is no closed history but a living warning: what ruined them still lies in wait for whoever follows their way.

mots.racine: ن ب أ
mots.sens: has the tidings not come to you of

mots.racine: ذ و ق / و ب ل
mots.sens: they tasted the outcome of their conduct

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُ كَانَت تَّأْتِيهِمْ رُسُلُهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَقَالُوا أَبَشَرٌ يَهْدُونَنَا فَكَفَرُوا وَتَوَلَّوا ۚ وَّاسْتَغْنَى اللَّهُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ غَنِيٌّ حَمِيدٌ
versets.tr: dhālika bi-annahu kānat taʾtīhim rusuluhum bi-l-bayyināti fa-qālū a-basharun yahdūnanā fa-kafarū wa-tawallaw wa-staghnā llāhu wa-llāhu ghaniyyun ḥamīdun
versets.trad: That is because their messengers came to them with clear proofs, and they said: “Shall a mortal guide us?” They denied and turned away. God had no need of them; and God is Self-Sufficient, Worthy of all praise!
themes: the refusal of the mortal messenger
versets.commentaire: The scandal of the mortal guide (bashar): they refused that the light should pass through a man like themselves. Qushayrī stresses wa-staghnā llāh: God had no need of them — not from capricious disdain, but because He is al-Ghaniyy, the absolute Rich, who draws nothing from creatures and is in no way diminished by their denial. Their turning away does not impoverish Him; it ruins only themselves.

mots.racine: ب ي ن
ب ش ر / ه د ي
غ ن ي
mots.sens: God had no need of them
mots.def: istaghnā: to be self-sufficient, to dispense with.

mots.racine: غ ن ي / ح م د
mots.def: ghaniyy: sufficient unto Himself; ḥamīd: praiseworthy.

versets.ar: زَعَمَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَن لَّن يُبْعَثُوا ۚ قُلْ بَلَىٰ وَرَبِّي لَتُبْعَثُنَّ ثُمَّ لَتُنَبَّؤُنَّ بِمَا عَمِلْتُمْ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ
versets.tr: zaʿama lladhīna kafarū an lan yubʿathū qul balā wa-rabbī la-tubʿathunna thumma la-tunabbaʾunna bimā ʿamiltum wa-dhālika ʿalā llāhi yasīrun
versets.trad: Those who deny claim that they will not be resurrected; say: “Yes indeed! By my Lord, you will be resurrected, then you will be informed of what you did: that is easy for God!”
versets.commentaire: To the “never” of denial (zaʿama — to claim without proof) comes an oath: “By my Lord, you will be resurrected.” Qushayrī: what unbelieving reason judges impossible is yasīr, easy, for the One whose power knows neither effort nor limit. Resurrection is no second labour; to re-create is as simple as to create for the One to whom it suffices to say: “Be.”

mots.racine: ز ع م
mots.def: zaʿama: to claim, allege without ground.

mots.racine: ب ع ث
mots.sens: that they will not be resurrected
mots.def: buʿitha: to be resurrected, sent forth.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ر ب ب
mots.def: balā: yes indeed (affirmative reply to a negation).

mots.racine: ي س ر

versets.ar: فَآمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَالنُّورِ الَّذِي أَنزَلْنَا ۚ وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ
versets.tr: fa-āminū billāhi wa-rasūlihi wa-n-nūri lladhī anzalnā wa-llāhu bimā taʿmalūna khabīrun
versets.trad: So believe in God and His Messenger and the light We have sent down. God is well aware of what you do!
versets.commentaire: The light (nūr) that descends is the Qurʾān — and more secretly, per Qushayrī, the light of gnosis that God kindles in hearts. To believe “in the light” is not merely to assent to a Book, it is to open oneself to the radiance that dispels the darkness of the self. And God, khabīr, knows which heart kindles and which closes.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ر س ل
mots.sens: believe in God and His Messenger
mots.def: āmana: to believe, to have faith.

mots.racine: ن و ر / ن ز ل
mots.sens: and the light We sent down
mots.def: nūr: light; anzala: to send down.

mots.racine: ع م ل / خ ب ر

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يَجْمَعُكُمْ لِيَوْمِ الْجَمْعِ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمُ التَّغَابُنِ ۗ وَمَن يُؤْمِن بِاللَّهِ وَيَعْمَلْ صَالِحًا يُكَفِّرْ عَنْهُ سَيِّئَاتِهِ وَيُدْخِلْهُ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ
versets.tr: yawma yajmaʿukum li-yawmi l-jamʿi dhālika yawmu t-taghābuni wa-man yuʾmin billāhi wa-yaʿmal ṣāliḥan yukaffir ʿanhu sayyiʾātihi wa-yudkhilhu jannātin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā abadan dhālika l-fawzu l-ʿaẓīmu
versets.trad: The day He gathers you for the Day of Gathering will be the day of mutual loss and gain. For those who have believed in God and done righteous deeds, He will efface their evil actions and admit them into Gardens beneath which rivers flow; they will dwell therein forever. That is the supreme triumph!
themes: the day of mutual loss and gain
versets.commentaire: The word that gives the sūra its name: yawm al-taghābun, the Day of mutual loss and gain. Qushayrī, in the closing note of the Book, illumines it with a hadith: none enters Paradise without seeing the place he would have occupied in the Fire had he done evil — which increases his gratitude; and none enters the Fire without seeing the place he would have occupied in Paradise had he done good — which makes him feel his loss. The taghābun is this universal reckoning: each one measures at last the bargain he made of his life.

mots.racine: ج م ع
mots.sens: the day He gathers you for the Gathering

mots.racine: غ ب ن
mots.sens: the day of mutual loss and gain
mots.def: taghābun: mutual deception, where one gains what the other loses.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / س و أ
mots.def: kaffara: to efface, expiate; sayyiʾāt: evil deeds.

mots.racine: ف و ز / ع ظ م

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۖ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kafarū wa-kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā ulāʾika aṣḥābu n-nāri khālidīna fīhā wa-biʾsa l-maṣīru
versets.trad: But those who denied and called Our signs lies — those are the inhabitants of the Fire; they will dwell therein forever, and what a wretched destination!
versets.commentaire: To the supreme triumph corresponds, as in a mirror, the wretched destination. Qushayrī recalls that the denial of the signs (āyāt) is no mere error of opinion: it is the refusal of a heart that has seen and denied. The Fire is no arbitrary decree but the natural end of a life that refused the light offered in verse 8.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / أ ي ي
mots.def: kadhdhaba: to call a lie; āyāt: signs, verses.

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ن و ر
ب أ س / ص ي ر
mots.def: biʾsa: verb of blame; maṣīr: destination.

versets.ar: مَا أَصَابَ مِن مُّصِيبَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللَّهِ ۗ وَمَن يُؤْمِن بِاللَّهِ يَهْدِ قَلْبَهُ ۚ وَاللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: mā aṣāba min muṣībatin illā bi-idhni llāhi wa-man yuʾmin billāhi yahdi qalbahu wa-llāhu bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīmun
versets.trad: No calamity strikes save by the leave of God. Whoever believes in God, He guides his heart. God is the perfect knower of all things.
versets.commentaire: The pivot-verse of the sūra. Qushayrī: no calamity (muṣība) strikes outside the leave (idhn) of God — and whoever believes, God guides his heart: in ease toward gratitude, in trial toward patience and contentment (riḍā). Faith does not abolish calamity; it transforms the heart that receives it, so that what would shatter another heart orients his, instead, toward his Lord.

mots.racine: ص و ب
أ ذ ن
mots.sens: save by the leave of God

mots.racine: ه د ي / ق ل ب
ع ل م

versets.ar: وَأَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ ۚ فَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَإِنَّمَا عَلَىٰ رَسُولِنَا الْبَلَاغُ الْمُبِينُ
versets.tr: wa-aṭīʿū llāha wa-aṭīʿū r-rasūla fa-in tawallaytum fa-innamā ʿalā rasūlinā l-balāghu l-mubīnu
versets.trad: Obey God and obey the Messenger! If you turn away, [know that] it is only upon Our Messenger to deliver the message clearly.
themes: the charge of the message alone
versets.commentaire: Obedience to God and to His Messenger is a single obedience in two directions. Qushayrī recalls that the Messenger's mission stops at clear transmission (balāgh mubīn): he is not charged with hearts, but with the word carried to them. The listener's turning away does not tarnish the purity of the message; it binds only the one who turns away.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ر س ل
و ل ي
mots.def: tawallā: to turn away, turn one's back.

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ب ي ن

versets.ar: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa wa-ʿalā llāhi fa-l-yatawakkali l-muʾminūna
versets.trad: God! There is no god but He! In God, then, let the believers place their trust!
versets.commentaire: The heart of tawḥīd: lā ilāha illā huwa — there is no god but He. Qushayrī draws from oneness the trusting surrender (tawakkul): since there is no other than He, upon whom else should one lean? To acknowledge that He is the only God is already to lay into His hands the weight of every affair. Oneness believed by thought becomes tawakkul lived by the heart.

mots.racine: أ ل ه
mots.def: ilāh: deity; the formula of tawḥīd.

mots.racine: و ك ل / أ م ن
mots.def: tawakkala: to entrust oneself, rely on God.
mots.gram: lām of command + verb (form V) + subject

versets.ar: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّ مِنْ أَزْوَاجِكُمْ وَأَوْلَادِكُمْ عَدُوًّا لَّكُمْ فَاحْذَرُوهُمْ ۚ وَإِن تَعْفُوا وَتَصْفَحُوا وَتَغْفِرُوا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: yā ayyuhā lladhīna āmanū inna min azwājikum wa-awlādikum ʿaduwwan lakum fa-ḥdharūhum wa-in taʿfū wa-taṣfaḥū wa-taghfirū fa-inna llāha ghafūrun raḥīmun
versets.trad: O you who believe! Among your spouses and your children there are enemies to you! Beware of them! Yet if you pardon, overlook, and forgive, then God is indeed Forgiving, Compassionate!
versets.commentaire: The enemy (ʿaduww) is not always without: he may nest in the closest love, when attachment turns one away from God's right. Qushayrī: the warning calls not for harshness but for lucidity — and the verse at once tilts toward clemency: forgive (taʿfū, taṣfaḥū, taghfirū — three shades: pardon, turn the eyes away, forgive), for the servant who forgives imitates the Forgiver. He who wishes to be forgiven first learns to forgive his own.

mots.racine: ز و ج / و ل د / ع د و
mots.sens: among your spouses and children an enemy

mots.racine: ح ذ ر
ع ف و / ص ف ح / غ ف ر
mots.def: ʿafā: to pardon; ṣafaḥa: to turn away from; ghafara: to forgive.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ح م

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا أَمْوَالُكُمْ وَأَوْلَادُكُمْ فِتْنَةٌ ۚ وَاللَّهُ عِندَهُ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: innamā amwālukum wa-awlādukum fitnatun wa-llāhu ʿindahu ajrun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: Your wealth and your children are but a trial, while with God there is a magnificent reward.
versets.commentaire: Wealth and children are a fitna: not an evil in themselves, but a trial by fire, like gold tested in the crucible. Qushayrī: they tempt the heart by vying for the place of dhikr. The counterpoint is given at once — with God a reward immense: whoever does not enslave his heart to his wealth finds with God something vaster than all he could have hoarded.

mots.racine: م و ل / و ل د / ف ت ن
mots.sens: your wealth and children are a trial

mots.racine: أ ج ر / ع ظ م

versets.ar: فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُمْ وَاسْمَعُوا وَأَطِيعُوا وَأَنفِقُوا خَيْرًا لِّأَنفُسِكُمْ ۗ وَمَن يُوقَ شُحَّ نَفْسِهِ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: fa-ttaqū llāha mā staṭaʿtum wa-smaʿū wa-aṭīʿū wa-anfiqū khayran li-anfusikum wa-man yūqa shuḥḥa nafsihi fa-ulāʾika humu l-mufliḥūna
versets.trad: So fear God as much as you are able! And listen, obey, and give in alms! That is best for you! Those who are guarded against their own greed — those are the ones who prosper.
versets.commentaire: “Fear God as much as you are able” (mā istaṭaʿtum): taqwā is not demanded beyond one's strength, but to the full measure of one's strength. Qushayrī dwells on deliverance from the soul's avarice (shuḥḥ al-nafs): that clenched greed which prevents giving. To be guarded from it — that is success (falāḥ): not to possess, but to be freed from the possession that possesses.

mots.racine: و ق ي / ط و ع
mots.sens: fear God as much as you can
mots.def: ittaqā: to fear piously; istaṭāʿa: to be able.

mots.racine: س م ع / ط و ع / ن ف ق
mots.def: samiʿa: to listen; anfaqa: to spend.

mots.racine: و ق ي / ش ح ح
mots.sens: who is guarded against his soul's greed

mots.racine: ف ل ح
mots.sens: they are the ones who prosper

versets.ar: إِن تُقْرِضُوا اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا يُضَاعِفْهُ لَكُمْ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ۚ وَاللَّهُ شَكُورٌ حَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: in tuqriḍū llāha qarḍan ḥasanan yuḍāʿifhu lakum wa-yaghfir lakum wa-llāhu shakūrun ḥalīmun
versets.trad: If you lend to God a goodly loan, He will multiply it for you and forgive you. God is Grateful, Forbearing;
versets.commentaire: The overwhelming image of the qarḍ ḥasan, the goodly loan: the absolute Rich makes Himself the borrower of His servant. Qushayrī: the rich man lends of his wealth, but the poor man lends the stripping-bare of his own will — emptying his wanting into the hands of God. And God names Himself al-Shakūr, the Grateful: He thanks the servant for the little He Himself first gave him. What generosity, to declare oneself indebted for what one has oneself bestowed.

mots.racine: ق ر ض / ح س ن
mots.sens: if you lend God a goodly loan

mots.racine: ض ع ف
mots.sens: He will multiply it for you

mots.racine: ش ك ر / ح ل م
mots.def: shakūr: most grateful; ḥalīm: gentle, patient.

versets.trad: He knows the hidden and the manifest; He is the Mighty, the Wise!
themes: knowledge of the hidden and the seen
versets.commentaire: The sūra closes on four Names that sum up its whole course: ʿĀlim al-ghayb wa-l-shahāda — the Knower of the hidden (ghayb) and the manifest (shahāda); al-ʿAzīz, the Mighty whom nothing constrains; al-Ḥakīm, the Wise whose every decree has its measure. He who opened the sūra with the universal glorification seals it with His total knowledge: nothing escapes the One who sees the inside of hearts and the beyond of times.

mots.racine: ع ل م / غ ي ب / ش ه د
mots.sens: knower of the hidden and the seen
mots.def: ghayb: the unseen; shahāda: the manifest.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
